titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sidney B. Kramer Papers and Business Records
Kramer (Sidney B.) Papers and Business Records
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 10, 2024 at 03:02

did.unittitle: Sidney B. Kramer papers and business records

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, business records, artwork, merchandise, audiovisual material, and other personal and professional papers relating to literary agent Sidney B. Kramer and his representation of author and illustrator Richard Scarry.

bioghist.head: Sidney B. Kramer (1915–2014)
bioghist.p: Sidney B. Kramer was an American publisher, literary agent and attorney who represented children's book author and illustrator Richard Scarry from 1965 to the 1980s. Born in New York, New York in 1915, Kramer graduated from New York University in 1936 and Brooklyn Law School in 1939. Kramer co-founded Bantam Books with Ian Ballantine, Betty Ballantine, and Walter B. Pitkin, Jr. in 1945, serving as its vice-president and director until 1967. He subsequently worked for Corgi Books and Cassell & Collier MacMillan in London, England and served as president of New American Library from 1967 to 1972.

Kramer established the literary agency MEWS Books Ltd., where he represented children's book authors such as Richard Scarry, Hardie Gramatky, Crockett Johnson, and Ruth Krauss. In addition to his publishing career, Kramer operated the Remarkable Bookshop in Westport, Connecticut with his wife Esther, where he also founded his own law firm. The couple was married in 1939 and had two children. Kramer died in Westport in 2014.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, business records, artwork, merchandise, audiovisual material, and other personal and professional papers relating to literary agent Sidney B. Kramer and his representation of author and illustrator Richard Scarry. Scarry's artwork includes original drawings, watercolors, and bluelines for his children's books. Merchandise includes toys, handkerchiefs, jigsaw puzzles, and party supplies featuring well-known Scarry characters such as Lowly Worm. Other business records pertain to Scarry's wife Patricia M. Scarry and son Huck Scarry, both of whom were also authors, as well as Kramer's other clients at MEWS Books Ltd., including Hardie Gramatky, Crockett Johnson, and Ruth Krauss. The collection also contains legal documents and personal files belonging to Kramer. Correspondents include publisher Ole Risom.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Chronological Files, 1972–2008. II. Business Records, circa 1965–2013. III. Artwork, 1941–1967. IV. Merchandise, 1971–1978. V. Legal Files, 1965–2014. VI. Personal Papers, 1966–2008. VII. Audiovisual Material, 1979–1984.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 39 (personal correspondence): Restricted until 2059. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 55, 61, 65-66: In-process material pending housing. For further information, consult Access Services.
Boxes 72-73 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
January 2020 acquisition: Restricted until 2045. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sidney B. Kramer Papers and Business Records. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sidney B. Kramer Papers and Business Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains assorted personal and business correspondence, carbon copies of agreements, contracts, memoranda, and other professional papers pertaining to Richard Scarry and Sidney B. Kramer. Included are letters exchanged by Scarry and Kramer, as well as correspondence with publishers, agents, manufacturers, and licensees of Scarry's works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains business records related to Sidney B. Kramer's literary agency MEWS Books Ltd. and its clients. Files pertaining to Richard Scarry consist of business correspondence, agreements, contracts, financial papers, royalties, tax records, publicity files, wills, notes, photographs, and other papers. The bulk of material documents Scarry's publishing and merchandising ventures, including foreign publishing contracts, licensing and copyright, and television rights. Notable correspondents include publisher Ole Risom. Present are page proofs for What Do People Do All Day?

Files pertaining to Huck Scarry consist of personal and professional correspondence, agreements, publishing contracts, and other business records. Included are watercolors and page proofs for his unpublished picture book "Cerise and Whiskers." The series also contains files related to his father Richard Scarry's estate.

Files pertaining to Patricia M. Scarry include personal letters and postcards exchanged with Kramer. Also present are publishing contracts for her children's books, and professional correspondence with other writers, illustrators, and publishers.

Files pertaining to Kramer's other literary clients document his representation of Hardie Gramatky, Crockett Johnson, Ruth Krauss, and other writers. Files include professional correspondence, typescript drafts, and publishing contracts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Richard Scarry Files, Huck Scarry Files, Patricia M. Scarry Files, and Other Client Files. Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by folder title, with oversize materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: What Do People Do All Day? , poster

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 39: Restricted until 2059. For further informatino consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2059. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains drawings, watercolors, and paintings by Richard Scarry. Included are bluelines and proofs for several of Scarry's picture books, among them What Do People Do All Day? and the Tinker and Tanker series. Also present is a watercolor by Huck Scarry.

did.unittitle: The Early Bird , bluelines and related drawings
The Early Bird , bluelines and related drawings
Edward Lear's Nonsense Alphabet , watercolor end sheets
The Hickory Dickory Dock Book , watercolor cover proof
"There Was an Old Man with a Beard" and "There was an Old Man on whose nose…" by Edward Lear, watercolor
Tinker and Tanker and the Pirates , envelope
Tinker and Tanker and the Pirates , proofs and related drawings
Tinker and Tanker and Their Space Ship , proofs
Tinker and Tanker in Africa , proofs
Tinker and Tanker in Africa , proofs
Tinker and Tanker: Knights of the Round Table , proofs and related drawings
Tinker and Tanker Out West , proofs and related drawings
Untitled colored pencil drawing, whale and seagull
Untitled [gouache?] painting on paper, swordfish in boat
Untitled oil painting on canvas, sailor

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 55: In-process material pending housing. For further information, consult Access Services.

did.unittitle: Untitled watercolor, boy with dog and telescope
What Do People Do All Day? , bluelines
What Do People Do All Day? , bluelines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains merchandise related to Richard Scarry's children's books, including character toys for Lowly Worm and others, handkerchiefs, jigsaw puzzles, party supplies and stationary sets. Merchandise was produced for the United States, Italy, and Japan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "Down on the Farm" paper plates by Hallmark
"Down on the Farm" party invitations by Hallmark
"Down on the Farm" party napkins by Hallmark
Lowly Worm nut cups by Hallmark

Children's briefcase by Auguri di Mondadori, Italy

did.unittitle: Golden Book Best Word Book Ever frame-tray puzzles

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 66: In-process material pending housing. For further information, consult Access Services.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains estate administration records, wills, and other legal documents generated during Sidney B. Kramer's legal practice. Included are files pertaining to the estate of Hardie Gramatky.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Sidney B. Kramer's personal correspondence unrelated to Richard Scarry, as well as files pertaining to investments, leases, and other papers for the Remarkable Bookshop.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains audiocassettes, digital data pack cassettes, and Super 8 film related to Richard Scarry.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 72-73 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Box 72: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
